observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct:
a. Review and analyze the transactions about the affected accounts with abnormal balances to comply with the established debit and credit balance in the Chart of Accounts provided in Chapter 2 of MNGAS, Volume III; b. Record the necessary adjustments in the books to present accurate and reliable balances of the accounts in the financial statements; and c. Properly monitor the transactions and entries to avoid abnormal balances of the accounts.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the Intra-agency accounts, prepare the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct account balances of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds, and present reliable data in the financial statements, under Section 111(2) of Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1444.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Expedite the reconciliation of the documents relative to the abnormal/negative balances in the subsidiary ledgers of the Due to NGAs and Due to LGUs accounts with a total debit balance of ₱375,167.86; and
b. Prepare the necessary adjusting entries in accordance with Item 7.3 of Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2016-005, to reflect in the financial statements the correct balance of the Due to NGAs and Due to LGUs accounts.

We recommended and the Local Chief Accountant to:
a. Exhaust all efforts to retrieve the supporting documents relative to the long outstanding book reconciling items of the General, Trust and Special Education Funds; b. Prepare the journal entries to record the reconciling items that require adjustment and correction on the GL in accordance with Section 3.3 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996; and c. Make representation with the authorized government depository bank to obtain details on the unadjusted debit and credit memoranda of the LGU’s bank accounts; and

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Submit the General Ledger, together with the Subsidiary Ledgers of the Payable accounts; and
b. Prepare the detailed aging schedules of the payable accounts to identify the balances and number of years the payables have been outstanding in compliance with Section 10, Chapter 1 of the MNGAS for LGUs and Section 3.1 of COA Accounting Circular Letter No. 2007-002.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. To record the bank account balance of San Dionisio MHO and Birthing Facility in the books, and the corresponding payable account, to comply with Section 112 of P.D. No. 1445;
b. To prepare monthly the bank reconciliation statement for the San Dionisio MHO and Birthing Facility bank account, in compliance with Section 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 6, 1996; and
c. To submit the copies of the BRS to the Auditor within 10 days from receipt of the Bank Statement after the end of each month, including the paid checks, original copies of the debit and credit memo in accordance with Section 3.4 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 6, 1996.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit to the Audit Team:
a. The Subsidiary Ledgers of the Due from Officers and Employees account in accordance with Section 10, Chapter 1 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II; and
b. The detailed aging schedules of the Due from Officers and Employees account to identify the balances and number of years the receivables have been outstanding in compliance with Section 111 (1) of Presidential Decree No. 1445.

We reiterated the recommendation and the LCE agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to make representations or issue communications with the concerned agencies to reconcile and verify the correctness of the receivable accounts balances and make the necessary adjustments if warranted. In addition, we recommended and the LCE agreed to file a request for authority to write-off dormant receivable accounts as provided under COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the GSO-designate to:
a. Accomplish the Property Acknowledgement Receipt (PAR) form for all of the PPE issued in accordance with Section 6.3.1 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, in order to determine the accountable persons and establish accountability; and
b. Observe proper filing of the issued PARs to avoid misplacement of the files.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive to:
a. Continuously send demand letter to require the previous Municipal Treasurer to produce the undeposited collections and deposit the same with the Authorized Government Depository Bank (AGDB) and to provide explanation why the said amount was undeposited as of December 31, 2023 or to, if necessary, initiate proper proceedings; b. Require the collecting officers to produce the undeposited collections and deposit the same with the AGDB; and c. Instruct all Accountable Officers to deposit intact all the collections with the AGDB daily or not later than the next banking day as required under Section 69 of P.D. No. 1445 and Section 32 of MNGAS for LGUs Volume I.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct:
a. Exhaust efforts to ascertain the Source Agencies and make representations or issue communications thereof to verify the correctness of the payable accounts balances and require the return of the unexpended balance as the case may be, in accordance with COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994. Otherwise, make the necessary adjusting journal entries in the books; and
b. Expedite the review of dormant unliquidated fund transfers and facilitate the filing of a request for authority to write-off, if warranted, as provided under COA Circular 2016-005.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:
a. Create a Disposal Committee in accordance with Section 79 of P.D. No. 1445, to determine the serviceability of PPEs and facilitate the disposal process of the unserviceable properties; b. Direct the Disposal Committee to prepare the Waste Material WMR and Inspection Report (WMR) and IIRUP in consonance with Section 125 of MNGAS, Volume I; c. Instruct the General Services Officer/ Municipal Treasurer to request in writing for the disposal of the unserviceable properties, to the Office of the Auditor, in adherence with Section 167 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 22, 1992; and d. Require the Municipal Accountant, thereafter, to prepare necessary journal entries to derecognize from the books of account all the disposed of unserviceable properties to reflect the correct balance of the recorded PPE.

We recommended that the Management strictly enforce the collection of water fees from delinquent consumers by implementing the provisions of Municipal Ordinance No. 36-08 and Section 2 of P.D. No. 1445, especially on the collection policy and the penal provisions stated therein.

We recommend and the Management agreed to:
a. Establish sound internal control in the safeguarding of semi-expendable properties to properly account and monitor the assets under their accountability, in accordance with Sections 123 and 124 of P.D. No. 1445; and
b. Create a committee to investigate the whereabouts of the missing tangible items amounting to ₱0.675 million, and locate these assets, otherwise, hold the end-users accountable for the missing properties.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:
a. Designate an official/employee to:
a.1. Consolidate the PPA data from each department, and at the beginning of the year, provide the Office of the Auditor of the list of all on-going government PPA and those that are to be implemented during the year, consistent with Item 2.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013; and
a.2. Prepare and submit the quarterly Report on Government PPA, including all of the ongoing, for implementation, and fully implemented PPA, to the Audit Team; and
b. Direct all department heads concerned to prepare the list of all ongoing, for implementation, and fully implemented PPA and submit it to the designated official/employee for consolidation.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Establish the RPT/SET Receivables and the related Deferred income accounts at the beginning of the year based on the certified list of taxpayers and amount collectible submitted by the Municipal Treasurer in accordance with Section 20 of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) Volume I for LGU; and
b. Reconcile the recorded balance of the RPT and SET Receivables with the certified list of all real property tax delinquencies from the Municipal Treasurer and make necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct balances in the financial statements.

We reiterated, and the Management agreed on the recommendation to prepare a request for write-off of dormant unliquidated cash advances of the officials and employees who were already deceased as provided under COA Circular No. 2016-005.
We recommended that the PEI granted in excess of Php5,000.00 to each of its officers and employees and the PEI granted to the NGAs personnel and those personnel assigned in the LGU be refunded.
The municipal officials and employees who received the extra cash gift for CY 2008 should immediately refund the same.

3.9 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to account for the discrepancy totaling ₱(30,618,570.12) between the Cashbook and Subsidiary Ledger on Cash in Bank- Local Currency, Current Account, and Cash – Local Treasury.
3.10 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to regularly reconcile their books to ensure that all entries in the books are complete and that any possible errors are immediately detected and rectified.

4.7 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Exert effort, devise a plan and take necessary steps in order to secure documents pertaining to the above-mentioned reconciling items to effect the adjustments appearing in the BRS to reflect the adjusted and correct balance of the Cash in Bank – Local Currency, Current Account; and
b. Reconcile the unaccounted discrepancies noted on the General Fund Cash in Bank – Local Currency, Current Account.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Record the PPE in the Trust Fund and record the transfer of the completed project from the Trust Fund to the General Fund, in compliance with Section 104 of the MNGAS for LGUs;
b. Provide the corresponding Depreciation Expense in accordance with IPSAS No. 17; and
c. Prepare entries to correct the understatement of the Accumulated Depreciation and the Government Equity accounts due to nonprovision of the Depreciation Expense of the completed project for CY 2023.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Prepare the necessary journal entries to correct the understatement of the Trust Liabilities-DRRMF, Cash-Local Treasury, and Subsidy to Other Funds and Accounts Payable accounts by ₱3.391 million; and
b. Adhere with COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012, on the correct recording of the transfer of the current year unspent QRF and DRRMF-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct:
a. The Municipal Accountant:
a.1. To follow the Perpetual Inventory Method in the accounting of inventories in compliance with Section 114 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for LGUs, Volume I; and
a.2. Prepare the necessary entries to correct the overstatement of the expenses and the understatement of the Inventory and Government Equity accounts.
b. The personnel responsible for the safekeeping of the relief goods, to prepare and submit the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued to the Municipal Accountant for the recording of expense.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Exercise prudence in granting overtime and observe the limitations and resort only to rendering overtime service when extremely necessary; and
b. Adhere with the guidelines set forth under CSC and DBM issued Joint Circular (JC) No. 1 s. 2015 dated November 25, 2015 consistent with CSC-DBM Joint Circulars No. 2, s. 2004 on the remuneration for overtime services through CTO.

We recommend and the Management agreed to:
a. Ensure that overnight travel claims were in the exigency of the service; and
b. Exercise sound management and prudence in the disbursements for traveling expenses, in accordance with Item 4 (8) of P.D. No. 1445.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Expedite the processing of the garbage vehicle Documents and provide manpower to operate the vehicle; b. Direct the responsible officers to:
b.1. Implement the provisions of R.A. No. 9003 Use of a sanitary landfill or any other acceptable/authorized alternative types of dumpsites; b.2. Make representation with the DBM to inquire on the status of the financial assistance request of the LGU for the closure of the open dumpsite; and b.3. Facilitate the implementation of the safe closure plan of the dumpsite in accordance with Sections 17.h.3 and 37 of R.A. No. 9003

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to submit the CRAAF, together with the RAAF, to the Office of the Auditor, not later than the fifth day of the ensuing month, in accordance with Section 51 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs.

We recommended and the Management agreed to provide a budget for the formulation and implementation of the GAD Code. We further reiterated the recommendation and Management agreed to direct the concerned officials to formulate GAD Codes or GAD-related ordinances to advance women’s empowerment and gender equality.

observations.recommendation:
1.35. We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:

a. Instruct the General Services Office-Designate and the Inventory
Committee to conduct inventory of all PPEs and prepare the Report of
the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) by
Section 156 of COA Circular No. 92-386;
b. Direct the responsible officers to comply with the disposition
procedures for non-existing/missing properties, in compliance with
Item 7.0 of COA Circular No. 2020-006;
c. Direct the Municipal Accountant to:
b.1. Strictly follow the recording of a tangible item as PPE, if and only
if, it meets the definition and recognition criteria for PPE, which
includes the capitalization threshold of ₱50,000.00, following Item
No. 4.2 of COA Circular No. 2022-004 dated May 31, 2022;
b.2. Reclassify tangible items with acquisition cost of less than
₱50,000.00 initially classified as Property, Plant and Equipment
(PPE) to semi-expendable properties in accordance with Item
No. 4.1 of Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2022-004
dated May 31, 2022;
b.3. Reflect the correct Depreciation Expense and Accumulated
Depreciation of the PPE in accordance with Paragraph 71 of
International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) 17;
b.4. Draw journal entries to transfer the School Buildings amounting
to ₱4.099 million from the Trust Fund to the General Fund in
accordance with Section 104 (1) of the MNGAS for LGUs,
Volume I;
b.5. Correct the erroneous recording of PPE, semi-expendable
property and financial assistance;
b.6. Facilitate the reconciliation of the PPE Balance between the PPE
Ledger Cards and the Financial Statements; and
b.7. Make the necessary adjustments in derecognizing the non-
existing/missing assets in the books after the conduct of the
physical inventory and after the disposition procedures for the
said non-existing/missing assets were carried out.

d. Direct the GSO to prepare the Property Cards and reconcile the same
with the Accounting records, following Section 119, Chapter 7 of the
Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS)
Manual;
e. Provide additional manpower, when feasible, to assist in the
preparation and reconciliation of PPE records and reports;
f. Require the Inventory Committee, in compliance with COA Circular
No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, to:
e.1. Prioritize the preparation of the approved PIP and submit it to the
Office of the Auditor, at least 10 calendar days before the
scheduled start of inventory-taking activities; and
e.2 Conduct the one-time cleansing of the PPE to establish the correct
value of the account.

2.7. We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the
Municipal Accountant to:

a. Record the PPE in the Trust Fund and record the transfer of the
completed project from the Trust Fund to the General Fund, in
compliance with Section 104 of the MNGAS for LGUs;
b. Provide the corresponding Depreciation Expense in accordance with
IPSAS No. 17; and
c. Prepare entries to correct the understatement of the Accumulated
Depreciation and the Government Equity accounts due to nonprovision
of the Depreciation Expense of the completed project for CY 2023.

3.6. We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the
Municipal Accountant to:

a. Prepare the necessary journal entries to correct the understatement of
the Trust Liabilities-DRRMF, Cash-Local Treasury, and Government
Equity accounts by ₱3.391 million, and the overstatement of the Subsidy
to Other Funds and Accounts Payable accounts by ₱3.391 million; and
b. Adhere with COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012, on
the correct recording of the transfer of the current year unspent QRF
and DRRMF-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund.

4.10. We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal
Accountant to:

a. Expedite the retrieval of the supporting documents of the outstanding
cash advances and the reconciliation of the accounts with the supporting
documents;
b. Identify the accountable officers with unliquidated cash advances and
prepare the Subsidiary Ledgers and the detailed aging schedule of the
account;
c. Demand immediate liquidation of the ₱1.567 million from the
Accountable Officers with outstanding cash advances;
d. Withhold the AO's salary when no liquidation of the previous cash
advance is received on or before January 20 of the ensuing year, in
compliance with Section 5.9 of COA Circular No. 97-002;
e. Enforce strict compliance with the provisions of COA Circular
No. 97-002 and Section 89 pf P.D. No. 1445 on the grant and liquidation
of cash advances; and
f. Conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis and validation of
cash advances to determine long outstanding and dormant receivables
and prepare necessary reports together with the supporting documents
for possible request for write-off, if warranted, as indicated in COA
Circular No. 2016-005.

5.6. We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct:

a. The Municipal Accountant:
a.1. To follow the Perpetual Inventory Method in the accounting of
inventories in compliance with Section 114 of the Manual on the
New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for LGUs
Volume I; and
a.2. Prepare the necessary entries to correct the overstatement of the
expenses and the understatement of the Inventory and
Government Equity accounts.
b. The personnel responsible for the safekeeping of the relief goods, to
prepare and submit the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued to
the Municipal Accountant for the recording of expense.

6.5. We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct:

a. Review and analyze the transactions about the affected accounts with
abnormal balances to comply with the established debit and credit
balance in the Chart of Accounts provided in Chapter 2 of MNGAS,
Volume III;
b. Record the necessary adjustments in the books to present accurate and
reliable balances of the accounts in the financial statements; and
c. Properly monitor the transactions and entries to avoid abnormal
balances of the accounts.

7.6. We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the
Municipal Accountant to reconcile the Intra-agency accounts, prepare the
necessary adjustments to reflect the correct account balances of the Due
from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds, and present reliable data in the
financial statements, under Section 111(2) of Presidential Decree (P.D.)
No. 1444.

8.5. We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the
Municipal Accountant to:

a. Expedite the reconciliation of the documents relative to the
abnormal/negative balances in the subsidiary ledgers of the Due to NGAs
and Due to LGUs accounts with a total debit balance of ₱375,167.86; and
b. Prepare the necessary adjusting entries in accordance with Item 7.3 of
Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2016-005, to reflect in the
financial statements the correct balance of the Due to NGAs and Due to
LGUs accounts.

9.7. We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the
Municipal Accountant to:

a. Exhaust all efforts to retrieve the supporting documents relative to the
long outstanding book reconciling items of the General, Trust and
Special Education Funds; and
b. Prepare the journal entries to record the reconciling items that require
adjustment and correction on the GL in accordance with Section 3.3 of
COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

10.6. We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the
Municipal Accountant to:

a. Submit the General Ledger, together with the Subsidiary Ledgers of the
Payable accounts; and
b. Prepare the detailed aging schedules of payable accounts to identify the
balances and number of years the payables have been outstanding in
compliance with Section 10, Chapter 1 of the MNGAS for LGUs and
Section 3.1 of COA Accounting Circular Letter No. 2007-002.

11.7. We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the
Municipal Accountant to:

a. To record the bank account balance of San Dionisio MHO and Birthing
Facility in the books, and the corresponding payable account, to comply
with Section 112 of P.D. No. 1445;
b. To prepare monthly the bank reconciliation statement for the San
Dionisio MHO and Birthing Facility bank account, in compliance with
Section 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 6, 1996; and
c. To submit the copies of the BRS to the Auditor within 10 days from
receipt of the Bank Statement after the end of each month, including the
paid checks, original copies of the debit and credit memo in accordance
with Section 3.4 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 6, 1996.

12.12. We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:

a. Direct the Municipal Assessor’s Office to expedite the titling of all
parcels of land owned by the Municipal Government in compliance
with Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386 and furnish the
Municipal Accountant of the supporting documents for recording
purposes;
b. Create an Appraisal Committee, headed by the Municipal Assessor and
with representatives from each department concerned, to conduct the
appraisal of the land claimed as owned by the Municipality to be used
as basis for recording of the Land account balance;
c. The Inventory Committee to conduct an inventory of all the parcels of
land owned by the Municipality consistent with Section 5.1 of COA
Circular No. 2020-006 and reconcile with the records in the books; and
d. The Municipal Accountant to record in the books the land owned by
the Municipality upon receipt of the land titles, following Section 72 of
the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for
Local Government Units (LGUs).

13.7. We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the
Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit to the Audit Team:

a. The Subsidiary Ledgers of the Due from Officers and Employees
account in accordance with Section 10, Chapter 1 of the MNGAS for
LGUs, Volume II; and
b. The detailed aging schedules of the Due from Officers and Employees
account to identify the balances and number of years the receivables
have been outstanding in compliance with Section 111 (1) of
Presidential Decree No. 1445.

14.10. We reiterated the recommendation and the Local Chief Executive (LCE)
agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to make representations or issue
communications with the concerned agencies to reconcile and verify the
correctness of the receivable accounts balances and make the necessary
adjustments, if warranted.

14.11. In addition, we recommended and the LCE agreed to file a request for
authority to write-off dormant receivable accounts as provided under COA
Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:

Ensure that the members of the Inventory Committee are made aware of their designations for them to effectively carry out their respective duties and responsibilities;

Direct the Municipal Accountant to:

Make the necessary adjustments to restate the PPE balance following the capitalization threshold for PPE of ₱50,000.00, in accordance with Item 4.2 of COA Circular No. 2022-004 dated May 31, 2022;

The Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal Assessor, to prepare and furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year with a duly certified list of taxpayers showing the amount due and collectible for the year as reflected on the system; and
The GSO to prepare and submit the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued to the Municipal Accountant for the recording of expense.

The Municipal Treasurer to:

Ensure accuracy and completeness in the preparation of the Report of Checks Issued in accordance with Section 43 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs; and
Prepare and provide a copy of the list of unreleased checks to the Municipal Accountant at the end of the year.

The Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments for the unreleased checks at year-end, and for the stale checks, to restore the Cash in Bank and corresponding Payable accounts in the Financial Statements, in compliance with Section 59 of NGAS Manual for LGUs.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:

Ensure that funds were appropriated for the procurement of a vehicle compliant with the requirements set to transport residual wastes from the Municipality to the intended sanitary landfill;

Coordinate with the barangay officials to conduct IEC campaigns to inform the households on the segregation of wastes at source and establish focal persons in each barangay who shall be responsible in the monitoring of the implementation.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct: 

The concerned Rural Health Unit personnel to prepare the Inventory Report on the remaining undistributed vaccines as of year-end with information as to valuation and furnish the copy to the Office of the Municipal Accountant; and
The Municipal Accountant to record the transaction on the agency books based on the complete Inventory Report in compliance with COA Circular No. 2020-009.
The Municipal Treasurer to provide the Accountant of the details of the San Dionisio MHO and Birthing Facility to facilitate the recording in the books; and

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct:

The Municipal Accountant to adhere with the disclosure requirement of the unexpended balances of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund (LDRRMF) in the Notes to Financial Statements as prescribed in Item 5.1.16 of COA Circular No. 2012-002, in order to provide the necessary data and information to the users of the financial statements.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct:

The Municipal Accountant and the MDRRMO to reconcile the difference of ₱8,073.79 in the unexpended balance of the DRRMF as of December 31, 2022 as presented in the Report on Sources and Utilization of the DRMMF and in the Financial Statements in order to establish the accuracy of the unexpended DRRMF balance.

The Municipal Accountant and GSO maintain the required PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, and reconcile their property records.

The Municipality avail of the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts as provided under Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2020-006 to finally establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the GSO-designate to:

Require the previous Municipal Treasurer to produce the undeposited collections and deposit the same with the Authorized Government Depository Bank (AGDB) and provide explanation why the said amount was undeposited as of December 31, 2021 or if necessary, initiate proper proceedings; and

16.1 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) give priority on the implementation of LDRRMF PPAs in order to be prepared in case of disaster in the future and to avoid possible loss of life and property; identify additional PPAs for the unused/unutilized funds under the preparedness and mitigation aspect, and undertake steps to implement the PPAs prioritized to support disaster risk management activities such as pre-disaster preparedness programs including, among others, training, and procurement of life-saving rescue equipment and establishment of evacuation centers.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
1.1	Direct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to deposit all collections intact with the authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day as required under existing laws, rules and regulations;

1.2.	Instruct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to conduct regular reconciliation of the cash book and subsidiary ledger balances to ensure completeness and accuracy of the recorded transactions and the balance of Cash in Local Treasury account as of a given period;
1.3.	Demand immediate deposit of the remaining undeposited amounts; and
1.4.	Avail of possible legal remedies in regard to the accountability of the absconded Municipal Treasurer.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive;
2.1.	Direct the Municipal Accountant to request a copy of the missing bank statements from the authorized bank in order to itemize the reconciling item in the amount of ₱692,320.15;

4.1 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive create an Appraisal Committee headed by the Municipal Assessor and representative from each department concerned; direct the Committee to conduct the appraisal of the land claimed as owned by the Municipality; and make necessary reports in order for the Municipal Accountant to record and reconcile the balance of the Land account in the Financial Statement.

4.2 We also recommended that the management appropriate funds for the titling of all parcels of land owned by the Municipality; and the Accounting and Municipal Treasurer’s Office set up, maintain, and keep on file complete records of real properties.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
5.1. 	Direct the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical inventory of the Municipality’s properties and prepare and submit the corresponding inventory report;
5.2.	Require the Inventory Committee, the General Services Office and the Accounting Office to reconcile all the inventory reports for proper presentation in the Financial Statements; and

5.3.	Oblige the General Services Office to maintain complete PCs and the Accounting Office to prepare PPELCs and update them periodically to aid in the conduct of Annual Inventory Count and in the reconciliation of balances between the Inventory Report and the books of accounts.
6.2 We also recommended that the committee prepare and submit a report to the Municipal Accountant so that necessary adjusting entries can be made.

7.2 We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the accounting records against the report on the unserviceable properties and reclassify those  identified as unserviceable/obsolete properties to “Other Assets” account; and after the disposal, draw journal entry vouchers to derecognize the unserviceable properties and apply appropriate accounting procedures to effect adjustments in the books.

8.3. 	The management to instruct the office of the waterworks to consistently inspect/check the water meters and water lines and prepare the report every month in order for the management to regularly conduct repair and maintenance; and

10.2. 	Implement the existing Municipal Ordinance regarding the solid waste management system by adopting measures to observe the basic environmental regulations and effective application of the Municipality’s solid waste management programs, projects and activities, as planned, to totally achieve the objective of RA No. 9003 in protecting the environment and public health;
10.5. 	Strictly implement the policy on the use of personal protective equipment by the garbage collectors. An information campaign should be conducted to instill awareness in them.

11.2 We also recommended that the prior years’ unimplemented projects be reprogrammed to other priority projects that will conform to the present condition of the Municipality; and formulate a timetable that will determine the exact period when the project will be implemented as well as monitor the status of its implementation.

12.1 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) give priority on the implementation of LDRRMF PPAs in order to be prepared in case of disaster in the future and to avoid possible loss of life and property; identify additional PPAs for the unused/unutilized funds under the preparedness and mitigation aspect, and undertake steps to implement the PPAs prioritized to support disaster risk management activities such as pre-disaster preparedness programs including, among others, training, and procurement of life-saving rescue equipment and establishment of evacuation centers.
12.2 We also recommended that the LCE require the MDRRMO to prepare the LDRRMFIP with complete details of the PPAs to be funded out of the LDRRMF to facilitate prompt evaluation thereof.
13.2 We also recommended that the LCE provide additional staff in the Accounting Office to assist in the timely submission of accounts and reports.

14.2. 	Formulate GAD Codes or Ordinances that will support the LGU’s efforts in promoting, protecting and fulfilling women’s human rights, women’s economic empowerment and gender-responsive governance towards the attainment of gender equality and women’s empowerment;

15.1 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant and the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to make representations with the concerned national government agencies/ GOCCs concerning their records as against the books of the Municipality and make the necessary adjustments, if warranted.

19.1 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Local Poverty Reduction and Action Team to closely monitor the implementation of the projects that were identified and programmed under the Bottom-Up Budgeting and determine the reason/s for the late implementation of the projects.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

28.1 Ensure compliance with the documentary requirements on the liquidation of the cash advances totaling P1,500,000.00 by directing the submission of the lacking documents to the COA for evaluation;
34.1 We recommended that the concerned employees submit or comply with the deficiencies enumerated for evaluation.
36.1 The municipal officials and employees who received the extra cash gift for CY 2008 should be required to immediately refund the same.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE):
Demand the immediate deposit of the balances on undeposited collection of the previous liquidating officers;
Instruct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to monitor regularly the collections and deposits handled by the liquidating officers and require to reconcile the balances monthly in the office of the Municipal Accountant; and
Avail of possible legal remedies with regard to the accountability of the absconded Municipal Treasurer.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive demand the liquidation of the unliquidated cash advances and strictly comply with existing laws, rules and regulations COA Circular No. 97-002.
We also recommended that the Management should not allow the grant of new cash advance if liquidation of the previous cash advance is not submitted. Regular monitoring and reporting of the status of unliquidated cash advances shall be prepared by the Municipal Accountant and furnished the Local Chief Executive for his information and appropriate action.
We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant and concerned municipal employees conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis and validation of the cash advances and determine long outstanding and dormant receivable accounts. Identify status of all accountable officers and take necessary action to communicate to the current employers of those who were transferred or reassigned accountable officers. Lastly, prepare essential reports together with the supporting documents for the request for write-off as indicated in COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Agriculturist and the Municipal Engineer to submit report on the status of program/projects from which the inventories and breeding stock pertain copy furnished the Municipal Accountant. Direct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the account to proper asset/expense account upon receipt of the necessary report for proper recording of accounts in the Financial Statement.

We recommended that the Management:
Formulate specific guidelines on the grant of financial assistance within the purview of policies on the delivery of basic social services and the indigence program of the Municipal Social Welfare Development Office. The Municipality may adopt the DSWD MC No. 11 Series of 2019.
Budgets for implementation of approved programs, projects and activities of the Municipality be lodged to the proper offices under the Local Chief Executive.
Strengthen the policy on the grant of financial assistance to individuals. Identification of beneficiaries and amounts of grant be based on proper case evaluation by the social worker and reasonableness of the circumstances.

We recommended that the Municipality thru the Sangguniang Bayan should update regularly its Local Revenue Code pursuant to Section 129 and 191 of RA No. 7160 in order to increase the financial capability of the LGU in rendering basic mandated services.

We recommended that the LCE with all the members of the Municipal Planning and Development Council:
Include specific and identifiable PPAs for the 20% DF which are attainable within the desired time frame in order to maximize the allocated funds to have an immediate impact on the socio-economic development of the Municipality;
Prior years’ unimplemented projects be reprogrammed to other priority projects that will conform to the present condition of the Municipality and formulate a timetable that will determine the exact period when the project will be implemented as well as monitor the status of its implementation; and
Strictly adhere to the provisions of the DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1 in formulating PPAs in order to achieve the maximum benefits for its constituents.

We recommended that the management apply and/or renew the registration of all serviceable motor vehicles of the Municipality with LTO and contracts of insurance with the GSIS. Regularly monitor the expiration of all motor vehicles registration and insurances and its timely renewal/registration to avoid invalidation and fines/penalties for late/non-registration and payment of damages in case of vehicular accidents.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to submit disbursement vouchers after payment with complete supporting documents to the Office of Municipal Accountant;
Require the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to the prescribed period for the submission of monthly and quarterly reports as well as transmitting all receipts and disbursement records with complete supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor; and
Provide additional staff in the Accounting Office to assist in the timely submission of accounts and reports.

We recommended that the Management:
3.1.	Direct all the concerned officials/employees to immediately settle the outstanding cash advances, and all advances must be liquidated at year-end;

3.2. 	Establish a strong internal control and create a policy or standard operating procedures in the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advance which provides for the sanctions/penalties for non-compliance thereon subject to existing relevant laws, rules and regulations;

3.3.	Instruct the Municipal Accountant to refrain from granting additional cash advances unless the previous advance is liquidated, and coordinate with the management of other government agencies to direct the officials/employees previously assigned in the Municipality to immediately settle their outstanding accounts; and

3.4.	Conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis and validation of the cash advances, determine long outstanding and dormant receivable accounts and prepare necessary reports together with the supporting documents for the request for write-off, if warranted, as indicated in COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended the following:
8.1. 	The management to strictly enforce the collection of water fees from delinquent consumers by implementing the provisions of Municipal Ordinance No. 36-08 especially on the collection policy and the penal provisions stated therein;

8.2. 	The offices concerned to coordinate properly for proper recording of actual financial status and data particularly on the recognition of income and receivables in the books in order to assist the management in better decision-making;
8.4. 	The management to enforce corrective measures and strictly impose sanctions, if warranted, in order to promote efficiency, economy and effectiveness in its operations.

We recommended that Management:
9.1.	Advise the Office of the Municipal Treasurer to conduct more intense action for the collection of delinquent accounts by prompting the issuance of notices to defaulting taxpayers and warrant of levy before the public auction;

9.2.	Coordinate/make representations with the Punong Barangays and seek their assistance to personally deliver the above notices/letters to concerned individuals in the locality and advise them to settle their accounts; and
9.3.	Seek advice from the concerned government agencies regarding applicable remedies on the CARP covered properties.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive create a committee to identify these PPEs and determine whether serviceable or not, and make necessary actions to provide information needed in the computation of depreciation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive prioritize the creation of the Committee on Award and immediately facilitate the disposal of unserviceable properties following the procedures prescribed in the aforecited laws and regulations to avoid further deterioration of the properties;  prior to disposal, recommendations of the COA Technical Services should be complied; and security must be provided in the locations of the unserviceable properties to avoid theft of its parts.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the accounting records against the report on the unserviceable properties and reclassify those  identified as unserviceable/obsolete properties to “Other Assets” account; and after the disposal, draw journal entry vouchers to derecognize the unserviceable properties and apply appropriate accounting procedures to effect adjustments in the books.

Seek advice from the concerned government agencies regarding applicable remedies on the CARP covered properties.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the persons concerned to immediately look into the unimplemented development projects under the 20% Development Fund and determine causes of non-implementation; and formulate/develop specific strategies/solutions to effectively implement the projects and prioritize what should be accomplished for its timely use without sacrificing the quality of work.
We also recommended that the prior years’ unimplemented projects be reprogrammed to other priority projects that will conform to the present condition of the Municipality; and formulate a timetable that will determine the exact period when the project will be implemented as well as monitor the status of its implementation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) give priority on the implementation of LDRRMF PPAs in order to be prepared in case of disaster in the future and to avoid possible loss of life and property; identify additional PPAs for the unused/unutilized funds under the preparedness and mitigation aspect, and undertake steps to implement the PPAs prioritized to support disaster risk management activities such as pre-disaster preparedness programs including, among others, training, and procurement of life-saving rescue equipment and establishment of evacuation centers.
We also recommended that the LCE require the MDRRMO to prepare the LDRRMFIP with complete details of the PPAs to be funded out of the LDRRMF to facilitate prompt evaluation thereof.
Furthermore, we recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant and MDRRMO to submit the monthly Report on Utilization of LDRRMF to the Auditor within the reglementary period using the prescribed format under COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to coordinate and submit timely the accounts and the required documents/reports of the Municipality to the Office of the Auditor to facilitate the audit and/or verification of its financial transactions and operations.

The OIC-Municipal Treasurer and all authorized collectors completely provide information required in the RCDs and regularly submit the CRAAF and RAAF to the Office of the Auditor for monitoring of the receipts and issuance of accountable forms; and

Direct the establishment of the Municipal GFPS that shall spearhead the preparation of the Annual GPB and GAD Accomplishment Report; and
Ensure that the provisions of the PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 are strictly observed.
1.1We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer prepare and submit the SCBAA as of December 31, 2015 to show whether the LGU was compliant with its approved budget.

2.1We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the 
OIC-Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the BRS monthly to the Auditor, within ten (10) days after receipt of the BS.
2.2We also recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant secure print-out or snap-shot copy of the BS as an alternative procedure in preparing the BRS on time.
2.3Further, we recommended that the LCE provide additional staff to the OIC-Municipal Accountant to assist him in the preparation of the financial reports and supporting schedules/documents.
Close monitoring of cash advances by the OIC-Municipal Accountant to avoid additional grant of cash advance to officers and employees with unliquidated cash advances; and
observations.reason: The newly assumed Municipal Accountant closely monitor the grant of cash advance

observations.recommendation: 7.1 We recommended that the concerned municipal officials strictly adhere to the rules and regulations of RA No. 5447, Section 272 of the Local Government Code of 1991 and DECS-DBM-DILG Joint Circular Nos. 01 s. 1998, 01-A and 01-B on the utilization of the Special Education Fund.

7.2 We further recommended that the Local School Board prioritize programs, projects and activities as stipulated in the aforementioned provisions in the preparation of the School Board Budget for the SEF.

Direct management personnel in charge of the LDRRMF to strictly adhere to the provisions of RA No. 10121 and its IRR, COA Circular No. 2012-002 and other related laws and regulations on the disbursements of the LDRRMF.

Observe fiscal responsibility in all financial transactions of the Municipality, to be exercised by all officers and employees, by ensuring that sufficient/ relevant documents are attached to the reports to show that expenses are for public purpose and that the claims are valid.
14.1 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit subsidiary ledgers or detailed schedules of his working papers on the conversion of accounts to ascertain its correctness.

115.1 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit all collections intact with the authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day as required under existing regulations of the Commission on Audit.
observations.reason: Large portion of the cash in vault balance as of year-end is the accountability of the absconded Municipal Treasurer who failed to account for her collections.

observations.recommendation: 17.1 We recommended that the PEI granted in excess of PhP5,000.00 to each of its officers and employees and the PEI granted to the NGAs personnel assigned in the LGU and those personnel excluded in the BC Circular  be refunded.
observations.reason: Refunds were partially made thru deduction from the monthly salary of each employee

observations.recommendation: 117.2 We further recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan strictly observe the PS limitation provided in Section 325(a) of RA No. 7160, and EO No. 80 and DBM Budget Circular No. 2014-3 in the grant of PEI.

18.1 We recommended that the LGU (1) formulate uniform policies and procedures in the procurement process in accordance with the relevant provisions of RA No. 9184, its Revised IRR and other relevant laws, rules and regulations; (2) prepare the Annual Procurement Plan through the BAC secretariat; (3) use public bidding as a general mode of procurement and avail only the alternative method of procurement if the circumstances warrant; (4) stop the use of reimbursement in purchasing office supplies and other goods and services; (5) strictly comply with the pertinent provisions of RA No. 9184 and its IRR.
observations.reason: The Municipality prepared the APP for CY 2015 but was not followed because some of the disbursements were reimbursements for the purchase of office supplies.

observations.recommendation: The GSO stop the existing practice in the procurement of gasoline/ fuel, oil and lubricant and observe the mode of procurement provided in RA No. 9184.
19.3 The Municipal Accountant verify the completeness of the supporting documents before certifying on the vouchers.

20.1We recommended that copy of any contract, letter order, job order or purchase order irrespective of amount, and all its supporting documents shall be submitted to the Auditor within five (5) working days from issuance thereof to facilitate review and evaluation.
observations.reason: The Municipality was only partially compliant of the recommendations since records showed that not all PO's were submitted.

observations.recommendation: 23.1We recommended that the agency fast tract implementation of identified development projects covered by the ₱9.04M funds intended for CY 2012 in accordance with Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1, dated April 13, 2011. Henceforth, efforts should be exerted to implement all projects funded out of the 20% Development Fund during the year where identified.

26.1 We recommended that the Municipality exert effort to determine how these lands were acquired and immediately act to effect issuance of Certificates of Title (Torrens Title) or Deeds of Donation, as the case maybe.  This will preclude the occurrence of disputes over the use of the property.  The properties will be safeguarded against possible loss or unlawful occupants/squatters.
observations.reason: Lack of available manpower to perform the function.

observations.recommendation: 26.2 The Municipal Mayor, being immediately responsible for the proper and effective use of these properties should assign one personnel to be directly responsible and accountable to him for all the documentary requirements and safekeeping of real property records.
